message.content:
You are a web research agent. Research the DeepResearch Bench paper and its RACE evaluation framework. Use WebSearch and WebFetch (you must load them via ToolSearch with query "select:WebSearch,WebFetch" first).

Target sources:
- arXiv 2506.11763 (DeepResearch Bench paper) — abstract, PDF, and any HTML version
- The DeepResearch Bench leaderboard (github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench or the HuggingFace space)
- Any blog posts explaining RACE (Reference-based Adaptive Criteria-driven Evaluation)

Find and report CONCRETELY, with direct quotes and source URLs:
1. What exactly is RACE? How does it score? What are the four dimensions (Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability) and their exact weights? Confirm the weights (I believe ~0.32 Insight, 0.29 Comprehensiveness, 0.25 Instruction-Following, 0.14 Readability but verify).
2. Is RACE reference-based (compares against a reference report)? Is it comparative? Does it strip bibliography/citations before judging prose? What model is the judge (Gemini?)?
3. The FACT metric — what does it measure (citation accuracy / support)?
4. The current leaderboard: exact scores for top systems as of mid-2026. Report the ranked list with Overall RACE scores. Especially: Baidu Qianfan DeepResearch, ZTE-Nebula, Zhipu/GLM deep research, NVIDIA AI-Q, Doubao, Kimi Researcher, Gemini/OpenAI/Perplexity deep research, and where a ~0.49-0.51 system would rank.
5. Any published analysis of WHAT distinguishes high-scoring from low-scoring reports on this benchmark.

Return a detailed structured summary with exact numbers and source URLs. Mark anything you cannot verify as "unknown". Do not guess.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.prompt: Extract the full abstract and any details about the RACE evaluation framework, its four dimensions (Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, Readability), their exact weights, whether it is reference-based and comparative, what judge model is used, and the FACT metric. Report exact numbers and quotes.

input.query: DeepResearch Bench RACE evaluation dimensions weights Comprehensiveness Insight Instruction-Following Readability
DeepResearch Bench leaderboard RACE score Baidu Qianfan ZTE-Nebula Gemini Kimi 2026

content.content:
# DeepResearch Bench Paper Summary

## Full Abstract

"Deep Research Agents are a prominent category of LLM-based agents. By autonomously orchestrating multistep web exploration, targeted retrieval, and higher-order synthesis, they transform vast amounts of online information into analyst-grade, citation-rich reports--compressing hours of manual desk research into minutes. However, a comprehensive benchmark for systematically evaluating the capabilities of these agents remains absent. To bridge this gap, we present DeepResearch Bench, a benchmark consisting of 100 PhD-level research tasks, each meticulously crafted by domain experts across 22 distinct fields. Evaluating DRAs is inherently complex and labor-intensive. We therefore propose two novel methodologies that achieve strong alignment with human judgment. The first is a reference-based method with adaptive criteria to assess the quality of generated research reports. The other framework is introduced to evaluate DRA's information retrieval and collection capabilities by assessing its effective citation count and overall citation accuracy."

## RACE and FACT Framework Details

**The provided web content does not contain specific information about:**
- A RACE evaluation framework or its four dimensions
- Exact weights for any evaluation criteria
- Details about comparative methodology
- The specific judge model employed
- The FACT metric specifications

The abstract only mentions "two novel methodologies" involving reference-based assessment with adaptive criteria and citation evaluation, but the detailed framework specifications you requested are not included in this arXiv abstract page excerpt.

## DeepResearch Bench Leaderboard - RACE Scores

The Baidu Qianfan team submitted official evaluation results of Qianfan-DeepResearch on the DeepResearch Bench benchmark to the DeepResearch Bench Leaderboard. The evaluation covers 100 research tasks across 22 distinct fields.

Within the DRA (Deep Research Agent) category, Gemini-2.5-Pro Deep Research demonstrates leading overall performance on the RACE framework. OpenAI Deep Research also exhibits strong capabilities, surpassing Gemini-2.5-Pro Deep Research in the Instruction-Following dimension.

A major update added comprehensive evaluation of Kimi-Researcher, Doubao-DeepResearch, and Claude-Researcher. The evaluation infrastructure was upgraded with Gemini-2.5-Pro for RACE and Gemini-2.5-Flash for FACT evaluation.

However, the search results don't provide specific RACE score numbers for all the models you mentioned (Baidu Qianfan, ZTE-Nebula, Gemini, and Kimi). For the detailed numerical RACE scores, you may want to visit the DeepResearch Bench leaderboard directly or check the GitHub repositories referenced in the results.

Links: [{"title":"DeepResearch Bench: Benchmark for DRAs","url":"https://www.emergentmind.com/papers/2506.11763"},{"title":"Felo Research Test Report on DeepResearch Bench | Felo Search Blog","url":"https://felo.ai/blog/deepresearch-bench/"},{"title":"GitHub - Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench: DeepResearch Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Deep Research Agents · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench"},{"title":"DeepResearch Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Deep Research Agents [Quick Review]","url":"https://liner.com/review/deepresearch-bench-comprehensive-benchmark-for-deep-research-agents"},{"title":"AgentDisCo: Towards Disentanglement and Collaboration in Open-ended Deep Research Agents","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.11732"},{"title":"RACE: Adaptive Reference-Based Evaluation Framework","url":"https://www.emergentmind.com/topics/race-reference-based-adaptive-criteria-evaluation"},{"title":"Deep Researcher with Sequential Plan Reflection and Candidates Crossover (Deep Researcher Reflect Evolve)","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20843"},{"title":"DeepResearch Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Deep Research Agents","url":"https://deepresearch-bench.github.io/"},{"title":"Deep Research Bench Evaluation of NVIDIA AI-Q Blueprint — NVIDIA AI-Q Blueprint","url":"https://docs.nvidia.com/aiq-blueprint/1.2.1/evaluation/benchmarks/deep-research-bench.html"},{"title":"DeepResearch Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Deep Research Agents | AI Research Paper Details","url":"https://www.aimodels.fyi/papers/arxiv/deepresearch-bench-comprehensive-benchmark-deep-research-agents"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the DeepResearch Bench RACE evaluation framework:

## RACE Evaluation Framework

The RACE framework uses Gemini-2.5-pro to assess report quality based on four dimensions: Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, and Readability.

### Four Evaluation Dimensions

The four key dimensions are:
- **Comprehensiveness**: Coverage breadth and depth of the research topic
- **Insight/Depth**: Quality of analysis and insight generation
- **Instruction-Following**: Adherence to specific task requirements
- **Readability**: Clarity, organization, and presentation quality

### Dynamic Weights

The RACE framework dynamically generates task-specific weights and criteria, and operates through dynamic weight and adaptive criteria generation, reference-based scoring, and overall score calculation. 

A concrete example shows how weights vary by task: For an exemplar task evaluating the feasibility of investing in EV charging infrastructure, the Judge LLM assigned nuanced global weights such as Insight 0.35, Comprehensiveness 0.30, demonstrating that different tasks receive different weight allocations across the four dimensions.

By comparing target reports with high-quality references, RACE mitigates biases and ensures a more accurate evaluation of report quality.

# DeepResearch Bench: Leaderboard and Methodology

Based on the provided GitHub page, I cannot extract a complete leaderboard with exact RACE scores for all systems, as **the actual detailed numerical results table is not included in the page content provided**. The page references results but directs users to an external source.

## What the Page States:

The repository indicates that "View Latest Leaderboard: Visit our **DeepResearch Bench Leaderboard** for real-time updated evaluation results, detailed comparative analysis, and raw data"—pointing to an external Hugging Face leaderboard rather than displaying scores directly.

## RACE Methodology Details Available:

**Four Evaluation Dimensions:**
The RACE framework assesses reports across: "📚 **Comprehensiveness**: Coverage breadth and depth of the research topic; 🔍 **Insight/Depth**: Quality of analysis and insight generation; 📋 **Instruction-Following**: Adherence to specific task requirements; 📖 **Readability**: Clarity, organization, and presentation quality."

**Reference-Based Approach:** RACE "Compares target reports against high-quality reference reports to ensure discriminative evaluation" and uses "dynamic weights adapted to each task's specific requirements."

**Judge Model:** The page indicates GPT-5.5 is the current RACE evaluator (with GPT-5.4-mini for FACT), having replaced Gemini-2.5-Pro.

## FACT Metric:

FACT evaluates "Statement-URL Extraction" and "Support Verification," calculating "Citation Accuracy" and "Effective Citations."

**For exact leaderboard scores**, you'd need to access the external Hugging Face leaderboard link directly.

content.content:
# DeepResearch Bench: Leaderboard Data and Metrics

## RACE Overall Scores and Dimensions

The leaderboard presents results across four dimensions: Comprehensiveness (Comp.), Depth, Instruction-Following (Inst.), and Readability (Read.).

**Deep Research Agents:**
- Gemini-2.5-Pro Deep Research: 48.88 overall (48.53 Comp., 48.50 Depth, 49.18 Inst., 49.44 Read.)
- OpenAI Deep Research: 46.98 overall (46.87 Comp., 45.25 Depth, 49.27 Inst., 47.14 Read.)
- Perplexity Deep Research: 42.25 overall (40.69 Comp., 39.39 Depth, 46.40 Inst., 44.28 Read.)
- Grok Deeper Search: 40.24 overall (37.97 Comp., 35.37 Depth, 46.30 Inst., 44.05 Read.)

**Top LLM with Search Tools:**
- Claude-3.7-Sonnet w/Search: 40.67 overall (38.99 Comp., 37.66 Depth, 45.77 Inst., 41.46 Read.)

## RACE Dimension Weights

The document does not specify explicit numerical weights assigned to each RACE dimension, though ablation studies examine "criteria weights" and "dimension weights" as components.

## FACT Metric Definition

The framework assesses two components: Citation Accuracy (C. Acc.) and Effective Citations (E. Cit.). Gemini-2.5-Pro Deep Research achieved 111.21 average effective citations and 81.44% citation accuracy.

## Analysis of High vs. Low-Scoring Reports

The document notes that "different models excelled in different dimensions," but provides limited analysis distinguishing report quality factors beyond observing that reference-based comparison is "crucial for discriminative evaluation."

content.content:
# RACE Framework: Scoring Methodology

## Scoring Process
RACE employs a three-stage approach: dimension weighting, criterion generation, and reference-based scoring. The final score normalizes the target report against a reference using: "S_final(R_tgt) = S_int(R_tgt) / [S_int(R_tgt) + S_int(R_ref)]" producing values between 0 and 1.

## Four Dimensions
The framework evaluates outputs across four orthogonal quality aspects: Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, and Readability. However, **no fixed global weights are specified**—instead, "dimension weights are inferred independently across trials" through LLM judgment, then averaged.

## Reference-Based & Comparative
Yes, RACE is explicitly comparative. It benchmarks candidate reports "relative to a carefully curated reference solution" derived from validated Gemini-2.5-Pro Deep Research outputs (April 2025).

## Citation Stripping
Yes, citations are removed. The framework uses "a cleaning prompt" to ensure "the Judge LLM does not anchor scoring on superficial citation frequency or format."

## Judge Model
"Gemini-2.5-Pro" serves as the primary Judge LLM for RACE evaluation. The framework also references Gemini-2.5-Flash for related FACT benchmarking.

## Empirical Performance
On 50 Chinese-language tasks, RACE achieved "71.33%" pairwise agreement with human judgment, exceeding "average inter-human agreement of 68.44%."

toolUseResult.result:
# RACE Framework: Scoring Methodology

## Scoring Process
RACE employs a three-stage approach: dimension weighting, criterion generation, and reference-based scoring. The final score normalizes the target report against a reference using: "S_final(R_tgt) = S_int(R_tgt) / [S_int(R_tgt) + S_int(R_ref)]" producing values between 0 and 1.

## Four Dimensions
The framework evaluates outputs across four orthogonal quality aspects: Comprehensiveness, Insight/Depth, Instruction-Following, and Readability. However, **no fixed global weights are specified**—instead, "dimension weights are inferred independently across trials" through LLM judgment, then averaged.

## Reference-Based & Comparative
Yes, RACE is explicitly comparative. It benchmarks candidate reports "relative to a carefully curated reference solution" derived from validated Gemini-2.5-Pro Deep Research outputs (April 2025).

## Citation Stripping
Yes, citations are removed. The framework uses "a cleaning prompt" to ensure "the Judge LLM does not anchor scoring on superficial citation frequency or format."

## Judge Model
"Gemini-2.5-Pro" serves as the primary Judge LLM for RACE evaluation. The framework also references Gemini-2.5-Flash for related FACT benchmarking.

## Empirical Performance
On 50 Chinese-language tasks, RACE achieved "71.33%" pairwise agreement with human judgment, exceeding "average inter-human agreement of 68.44%."

Links: [{"title":"DeepResearch Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Deep Research Agents","url":"https://deepresearch-bench.github.io/"},{"title":"Felo Research Test Report on DeepResearch Bench | Felo Search Blog","url":"https://felo.ai/blog/deepresearch-bench/"},{"title":"GitHub - Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench: DeepResearch Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Deep Research Agents · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench"},{"title":"GitHub - langchain-ai/open_deep_research · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/langchain-ai/open_deep_research"},{"title":"VeriTrace: Evolving Mental Models for Deep Research Agents","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.26081"},{"title":"DeepResearch Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Deep Research Agents","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2506.11763"},{"title":"(PDF) DeepResearch Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Deep Research Agents","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/392717336_DeepResearch_Bench_A_Comprehensive_Benchmark_for_Deep_Research_Agents"},{"title":"Deep Researcher with Sequential Plan Reflection and Candidates Crossover (Deep Researcher Reflect Evolve)","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20843"},{"title":"Orchestrating Specialized Agents for Trustworthy Enterprise RAG","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.18267"},{"title":"GitHub - onyx-dot-app/onyx_deep_research_bench: Onyx Submission for Deep Research Benchmark · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/onyx-dot-app/onyx_deep_research_bench"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about the DeepResearch Bench leaderboard with RACE overall scores. Here's what the results show:

## Top Performers on DeepResearch Bench RACE Leaderboard

Gemini-2.5-Pro Deep Research demonstrated leading overall performance (48.88) across all dimensions, with OpenAI Deep Research following closely (46.98).

However, more recent results show:

ADORE achieved a score of 52.65, placing it at rank #1 on the leaderboard.

Felo Research achieved an Overall Score of 0.4937 (~49.37/100) on DeepResearch Bench — placing it ahead of leading competitors.

Regarding NVIDIA AI-Q (mentioned in your query), NVIDIA AIQ Research Assistant achieved an overall score of 40.52.

## Effective Citations (FACT Framework)

Deep Research Agents significantly outperformed LLMs with Search Tools in terms of Effective Citations.

The specific models you mentioned (ZTE-Nebula, Qianfan, and GLM) were not prominently featured in the top-ranked results I found. The leaderboard appears to be dominated by services like ADORE, Felo Research, Gemini-2.5-Pro Deep Research, and OpenAI Deep Research based on the most recent data available.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.
